Politics.headline: Trump Administration Signals Mulling NATO Withdrawal After Iran War
Politics.detail: Amidst the aftermath of the Iran war, the Trump administration is reportedly considering withdrawing from NATO, raising concerns about the future of the alliance.

Politics.headline: Ceasefire is threatened as Israel expands Lebanon strikes and Iran closes strait again
Politics.detail: A ceasefire deal to pause the war in Iran appears to be hanging by a thread after the Islamic Republic closed the Strait of Hormuz again in response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon.

Politics.headline: UK blocks rapper Kanye West from entry over anti-Semitism and Nazi support
Politics.detail: British PM Keir Starmer said it was concerning that London's Wireless Festival had booked West in the first place.

Politics.headline: Governor Green Authorizes Relief for Kona Low-Impacted Taxpayers
Politics.detail: Hawaii Governor Josh Green has authorized the Department of Taxation to provide relief to eligible residents and businesses impacted by the Kona Low storms, extending the state income tax filing and payment deadline to July 20, 2026.

Politics.headline: Clackamas County District Attorney's office looks to spend on new AI tools
Politics.detail: Clackamas County commissioners are set to vote April 9, 2026, on whether to approve a contract enabling the use of an artificial intelligence tool in the county prosecutor's office. The 10-year $2 million contract is included on the consent agenda for the commission's Thursday, April 9 meeting, meaning the board is not planning to discuss the proposal before taking a vote.

Finance & Economy.headline: Economic volatility 'somewhat' eases after US-Iran ceasefire: finance minister
Finance & Economy.detail: South Korea's Finance Minister Koo Yun-cheol stated that financial and foreign exchange market volatility has decreased following the two-week ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran, with South Korean stocks surging nearly 7 percent and the Korean won strengthening against the US dollar.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil prices plunge, stocks surge on US-Iran ceasefire
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil and gas prices plummeted, stock markets surged, and the US dollar weakened after the US and Iran agreed to a temporary ceasefire that may lead to the Strait of Hormuz reopening. The most widely traded oil contracts fell approximately 15 percent to around US$95 a barrel.

Finance & Economy.headline: CoBank Quarterly: Economic fallout of rising fuel and energy costs will be most acute in rural America
Finance & Economy.detail: Despite signs of a weakening labor market, the U.S. economy has continued to perform reasonably well, but surging energy costs and volatility in oil markets due to the Middle East conflict could alter the U.S. economic trajectory for the rest of the year. Grain farmers are experiencing new marketing opportunities due to energy market volatility, which is driving up grain and oilseed prices.

Finance & Economy.headline: Wall Street ready to 'tap into' Americans' retirement under new Trump rule: finance expert
Finance & Economy.detail: A financial expert warns that a new rule proposed by the Trump administration, intended to bolster retirement accounts, is a "massive greed grab for Wall Street" at the expense of Americans' retirement savings. The rule, called “Democratizing Access to Alternative Assets for 401(k) Investors,” would allow easier investment in alternative assets, but critics argue it primarily benefits Wall Street.

Finance & Economy.headline: Japanese Shares Dip on Ceasefire Uncertainty
Finance & Economy.detail: The Nikkei 225 Index fell 0.5% due to uncertainty surrounding the ceasefire agreement between Iran and the US-Israeli side, as a senior Iranian official claimed violations following Israeli strikes on Lebanon and Tehran continued to block the Strait of Hormuz. The dollar index remained below 99, near its lowest level in about a month, as the US-Iran ceasefire eased concerns about inflation and boosted expectations that the Fed could cut interest rates this year.

Technology.headline: Intel to join Musk on Terafab, lifting shares by 4.2%
Technology.detail: Intel Corp is joining Elon Musk's long-shot effort to develop semiconductors for Tesla Inc, Space Exploration Technologies Corp and xAI, marking a surprising twist in the chipmaker's comeback bid; Intel would help the Terafab project “refactor” the technology in a chip factory. Intel's shares jumped 4.2 percent to US$52.91.

Technology.headline: Meta unveils first AI model from costly superintelligence team
Technology.detail: Meta Platforms unveiled Muse Spark, the first artificial intelligence model from a team assembled last year to catch up with rivals in the AI race. The model will initially be available only on the lightly used Meta AI app and website but will replace existing Llama models powering chatbots on WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook and Meta's collection of smart glasses.

Technology.headline: Citigroup says AI helps speed account openings and systems upgrades
Technology.detail: Citigroup is using artificial intelligence to speed up account openings and the retirement of old software, the U.S. bank's head of technology, Tim Ryan, said. A system to process documents has shaved an hour off the time required to review documents before opening an account in the U.S. for the services division, to 15 minutes.

Technology.headline: Silicon Data brings transparency to future GPU prices for enterprise CFOs
Technology.detail: Silicon Data announced a new service called the GPU Forward Curve, offering a standardized look at the anticipated costs of GPU capacity, both now and in the future. The startup wants to address a growing headache facing almost every enterprise that's experimenting with AI as the demand for cutting-edge silicon has grown immensely and is beginning to outgrow the available supply, leading to rising prices.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Mysteriously Freezes Accounts for VeraCrypt, WireGuard, Windscribe
Technology.detail: Microsoft has been reportedly freezing accounts for users of VeraCrypt, WireGuard and Windscribe. The reason for the account freezes is currently unknown.

Science.headline: Webb Captures Striking Edge-On Views of Two Planet Nurseries
Science.detail: The James Webb Space Telescope has captured detailed edge-on views of two planet nurseries, providing insights into star and planet formation.

Science.headline: 'Oldest Fossil Octopus' Wasn't One After All
Science.detail: A famous fossil once believed to be the oldest known octopus has been re-evaluated and found to be a different animal.

Science.headline: Ancient Farmers Accidentally Created Aggressive “Warrior” Wheat
Science.detail: Research shows early wheat varieties developed aggressive traits unintentionally through farming practices.

Science.headline: Something Just Hit the Moon and Left a Bright New Scar
Science.detail: A recent impact event on the Moon has created a new, 22-meter-wide crater, highlighting ongoing changes to the lunar surface.

Science.headline: Mummified reptile hints at the origins of how we breathe
Science.detail: A recently discovered mummified reptile fossil is providing clues about the evolution of respiratory systems.

Sports.headline: Italian Football Tournament Boosts Winter Sports Lineup
Sports.detail: AC Milan, FC Internazionale Milano, Juventus, and Palermo FC are heading to Perth for Calcio Italiano, expected to attract thousands of visitors and inject millions into the WA economy.

Sports.headline: Medvedev smashes racket then bins it during 6-0, 6-0 loss in Monte Carlo
Sports.detail: Daniil Medvedev had a meltdown, smashing his racket during a shocking 6-0, 6-0 loss in Monte Carlo.

Sports.headline: Jos Buttler scripts history, enters elite T20 club
Sports.detail: Jos Buttler of Gujarat Titans reached a landmark in the IPL against Delhi Capitals, hitting his 600th six in T20 cricket, joining a list of only 4 other players to reach this milestone. His innings ended a 16-match wait for a T20 fifty.

Sports.headline: Falcons right tackle Kaleb McGary announces retirement after missing 2025 season with injury
Sports.detail: Kaleb McGary, the Falcons right tackle, has announced his retirement after missing the 2025 season due to an injury.

Sports.headline: Pirates sign teenage shortstop Konnor Griffin to a 9-year deal worth at least $140 million
Sports.detail: The Pirates have signed teenage shortstop Konnor Griffin to a nine-year deal worth at least $140 million.

Entertainment.headline: Great American Media Partners with Minivela to Develop and Distribute Micro-Drama Slate
Entertainment.detail: Great American Media and Minivela are partnering to launch Pure Flix Familia, a Spanish-language and bilingual destination for family-centered storytelling, focusing on mobile-first micro-dramas.

Entertainment.headline: Cineplex Inc. Announces Details of First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Webcast
Entertainment.detail: Cineplex Inc. will release its first quarter 2026 results on Monday May 11, 2026, and host an earnings webcast that morning at 10:00 am EDT to discuss the Company's results.

Entertainment.headline: Offset Sued, Give Us the Loot!!! Trying to Settle $100K Casino Tab
Entertainment.detail: Offset is being sued over an unsettled $100K casino debt.

Entertainment.headline: Wireless festival cancelled after Kanye West banned from entering UK
Entertainment.detail: The Wireless festival has been cancelled after Kanye West was banned from entering the UK.

Entertainment.headline: 'GoT' Actor Michael Patrick Dead at 35 After Battling Disease
Entertainment.detail: Actor Michael Patrick, known for his role in 'Game of Thrones,' has passed away at the age of 35 after a battle with an unspecified disease.

Crime & Justice.headline: New Hampshire suspect who shot officer and triggered massive manhunt killed in police gunfight
Crime & Justice.detail: Suspect who allegedly shot a New Hampshire police officer is killed in a gunfight after a massive manhunt that included a shelter-in-place order.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man caught on video wielding 13-inch kitchen knife at NYC grocery store moments before police shoot him
Crime & Justice.detail: Police shot a man who allegedly advanced at officers with a 13-inch kitchen knife outside a Manhattan grocery store, according to the NYPD on Monday.

Crime & Justice.headline: Detroit Man Sentenced to Five Years in Prison for Role in Interstate Drug Trafficking Operation Involving Large Quantities of Fentanyl, Heroin, and Cocaine
Crime & Justice.detail: A Detroit man has been sentenced to five years in prison for his involvement in an interstate drug trafficking operation dealing with large amounts of fentanyl, heroin, and cocaine.

Crime & Justice.headline: Prosecutors move to subpoena Tiger Woods' prescription drug records after Florida DUI arrest
Crime & Justice.detail: Prosecutors are seeking to subpoena Tiger Woods' prescription drug records following his DUI arrest in Florida.

Crime & Justice.headline: Doctor found guilty of trying to kill his wife during cliffside hike in Hawaii
Crime & Justice.detail: A doctor was found guilty of attempting to murder his wife by pushing her off a cliff during a hike in Hawaii.

Education.headline: Schools to remain closed for Assembly Elections 2026: Full state list
Education.detail: Many educational facilities will serve as polling stations for the Assembly Elections, with voting conducted in a single phase on April 9, 2026.

Education.headline: TAYLOR'S UNIVERSITY LEADS IN EDUCATION AND IMPACT
Education.detail: Taylor's University is making its mark globally, with 20 subjects ranked in the QS World University Rankings by Subject 2026, the highest among South-East Asia's private universities. This year also marks the debut of Law, Biological Sciences, and English Language and Literature in the rankings.

Education.headline: Open defense of the two PhD scholars from the Department of Education will be held on 9 April 2026
Education.detail: The Islamia University of Bahawalpur (IUB), Pakistan, will hold open defenses for two PhD scholars from the Department of Education on April 9, 2026.

Education.headline: News Headlines for School Assembly, April 9, 2026: India welcomes West Asia ceasefire
Education.detail: News headlines for school assemblies on April 9, 2026 include India welcoming the West Asia ceasefire and the RBI keeping the Repo Rate unchanged.

Education.headline: Elevate, support, and invest in the teaching profession
Education.detail: The Global Education Monitoring Report 2026 reveals that 273 million children and young people are out of school, and the share of qualified primary teachers has fallen in the Asia-Pacific region. More than half of countries have cut their education funding since 2015, leading to a crisis in education.

Lifestyle.headline: Another reason why you need to give 'dry January' a try
Lifestyle.detail: Performance coach Dan Lawrence says you can 'win the morning, win the day'.

Lifestyle.headline: US Doctor Explains Why Doing Everything Right But Not Losing Weight?
Lifestyle.detail: A US doctor explains possible reasons why people may struggle to lose weight even when following a healthy diet and exercise plan.

Lifestyle.headline: The woman helping to keep Japan's traditional crafts alive
Lifestyle.detail: Tina Koyama left Silicon Valley to build a future for Japan's handmade traditions, and her Kyoto-based studio works with more than 20 craft traditions and has turned cultural preservation into a viable business model.

Lifestyle.headline: Sarah Lahbati eyes Paris home worth over P300M in Netflix reality show
Lifestyle.detail: Actress Sarah Lahbati is considering purchasing a home in Paris, potentially worth over P300 million, as documented in a Netflix reality show.

Lifestyle.headline: Worries about flying seem to be taking off. Here's how to cope with in-flight anxiety
Lifestyle.detail: With anxieties about flying on the rise, this article provides advice on how to manage in-flight anxiety.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A gravely compromised DOH
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Department of Health (DOH) in the Philippines is facing controversies, especially during the Duterte years, which makes it a fragile institution.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: ​A respite: On the two-week U.S.-Iran ceasefire
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The U.S. and Iran have agreed to a two-week ceasefire and direct talks based on Iran's 10-point peace formula, following heightened tensions and a war that has impacted global economic stability.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Sara asks SC: Stop impeach hearings
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Vice President Sara Duterte has asked the Supreme Court to stop the House of Representatives' justice committee from hearing impeachment complaints against her, warning of a "bloodbath" if forced to defend herself.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Alarming deforestation
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Indonesia's conservation progress is threatened by a surge in deforestation due to ambitious state projects, with studies suggesting long-term damage outweighs short-term benefits.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: The higher-education degree trap
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Taiwan ranks second globally in terms of the share of the population with a higher-education degree, with about 60 percent of Taiwanese holding a post-secondary or graduate degree, according to a survey.

World.headline: Middle East Ceasefire Threatens to Unravel as Israel Assaults Lebanon and Iran Blocks Oil Tankers
World.detail: Despite a ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran, tensions remain high as Israel continues its attacks on Lebanon, and Iran reportedly blocks oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. The situation threatens to undermine the fragile truce and raises concerns about regional stability.

World.headline: Iran Accuses U.S. and Israel of Ceasefire Violations, Threatening Truce
World.detail: Iran alleges that the U.S. and Israel have violated the recently brokered ceasefire agreement, citing Israeli attacks on Lebanon and continued standstill in the Strait of Hormuz. These accusations raise concerns about the sustainability of the truce.

World.headline: India Welcomes West Asia Ceasefire
World.detail: Following the US and Iran's agreement to a two-week ceasefire, brokered by Pakistan, India has expressed its support for the move, hoping it will lead to lasting peace in the region. India's statement reflects cautious optimism amidst ongoing tensions.

World.headline: Nearly 200 Arrests in Türkiye After Attack on Israeli Consulate
World.detail: Turkish authorities have arrested nearly 200 individuals in connection with an attack on the Israeli consulate in Istanbul. The arrests come amid heightened tensions in the region following recent events.

World.headline: Ben Roberts-Smith Charged with War Crimes in Surprise Arrest
World.detail: Australia's most high-profile and decorated soldier, Ben Roberts-Smith, has been arrested and charged with multiple alleged war crimes over the killings of unarmed Afghan victims. The Victoria Cross recipient was arrested at Sydney Airport and will face court tomorrow.

Zimbabwe.headline: Botswana's President Boko to officially open ZITF 2026
Zimbabwe.detail: President Boko of Botswana is scheduled to officially open the Zimbabwe International Trade Fair (ZITF) 2026.

Zimbabwe.headline: CCC members jailed 22 years for Mbare political murder
Zimbabwe.detail: CCC members have been sentenced to 22 years in jail for a political murder that occurred in Mbare.

Zimbabwe.headline: Rights body witnessed intimidation, whip-wielding vetters at CAB3 hearings
Zimbabwe.detail: Reports indicate that human rights organizations observed intimidation tactics and the presence of whip-wielding individuals during hearings concerning Constitution Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3).

Zimbabwe.headline: ZRP Warns Public Against Boarding Private Vehicles
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has issued a warning to the public against using private vehicles for transportation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zanu PF unleashes 'terror' on 2030 dissenters
Zimbabwe.detail: Some victims said they had gone into hiding in urban centres, while others claim they are constantly on the move to evade the suspected ruling party members.
